task: A presentation/interaction polish sprint across the whole Studio Creator UI -- Screen 1, Screen 2, Workspace (the primary focus), a brand-new Object Strip, Publish, and a language pass -- explicitly not a capability, architecture, or Runtime change. Workspace gained a Home button + a read-only World/Page-Style header readout, a warmer header/sidebar/canvas/context-panel visual pass, and a real fix for a pre-existing latent duplicate-thumbnail race bug in renderList()/renderTimeline() plus an unrelated Publish Studio close-button CSS bug. The Page Strip stays vertical in the left sidebar (full functionality unchanged) per the brief's own explicit orientation instruction. The new Object Strip (js/objectStrip.js) is a horizontally-scrolling, child-friendly readout of every object on the current page -- Background/Artwork/Frame/Text/Stickers/Decorations -- each showing a thumbnail, friendly name, and Editable/Part-of-this-World status, fully bidirectionally synced with canvas and Context Panel selection via the exact same window.setSelectedSceneElement/setSelectedTextElement entry points the canvas's own click handlers use. A real language review replaced leaked technical vocabulary (Theme/Representation/Holder/Layer) with child-friendly words (World/Look/Frame/Order) across the sidebar, Theme Picker modal, World Designer tab, and Card Designer. See CLAUDE.md's Creator UI Convergence Sprint entry for the full account, including the Builder<->Creator mapping review's one disclosed, unfixed gap (Layer-Pack-declared decorative content has no Object Strip entry yet).
